content.text:
{"success":true,"process_rule_id":"J09B","process_rule":"1. From the decision table, copy all rows where the tour starts with `J09B` into a new working table named `J09B_temp`.

2. In table `J09B_temp`, add a new column `ENTSCHEIDUNG`.

3. In `J09B_temp`, classify the meter reading type and fill `ENTSCHEIDUNG` as follows:  
   - For each `HAUS`, count how many `EQUNR` are associated with it.  
   - Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to `"KSA"` when a `HAUS` has less than 3 `EQUNR`.  
   - Else set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to `"EVU"`.

4. Join `J09B_temp` with table `EABLG` using the `INSTALLATION` field, extending `J09B_temp` with the needed columns from `EABLG`.

5. Join `J09B_temp` with tables `NET_MASTER` and `SALES_MASTER` using the `EQUNR` field (JOIN table with NET_MASTER and SALES_MASTER table using `EQUNR`), so that each row in `J09B_temp` has the corresponding customer data from both master tables.

6. Compare, for each row in `J09B_temp`, the customer data fields sourced from `SALES_MASTER` and from `NET_MASTER` to determine whether sales customer data is present (i.e., the sales customer data is NOT empty).

7. For each row in `J09B_temp`, decide which customer data to use based on the comparison (XOR gateway “sales customer data NOT empty” / “else”):
   - if sales customer data is NOT empty for this row:  
     - Use the `SALES_MASTER` customer data table entries for this row as the final customer data (overwrite / fill the customer-related fields for export from `SALES_MASTER`).  
   - else:  
     - Use the `NET_MASTER` customer data table entries for this row as the final customer data (fill the customer-related fields for export from `NET_MASTER`).

8. Export the final data in `J09B_temp` as a CSV file named `J09B`, using exactly these column names (and this order) for the export (Use this column names, when exporting the file):  
   `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`, `HAUS`, `ANLAGE`, `ME_MA_ID`, `EQUNR`, `HOUSE_NUM1`, `ENTSCHEIDUNG`, `TITLE`, `FORENAME`, `SURNAME`, `STREET`, `STREETNO`, `POST_CODE1`, `CITY1`, `CITY2`, `EMAIL`.

9. Send an email with the exported CSV file to the EVU:  
   - Email address: `meter.readings@evu.com`  
   - Attach the CSV file located at `\<path_to_exported_csv_file\>`.

10. After the email has been sent successfully, delete the temporary working table `J09B_temp`.

11. End the process."}

structured_content.process_rule:
1. From the decision table, copy all rows where the tour starts with `J09B` into a new working table named `J09B_temp`.

2. In table `J09B_temp`, add a new column `ENTSCHEIDUNG`.

3. In `J09B_temp`, classify the meter reading type and fill `ENTSCHEIDUNG` as follows:  
   - For each `HAUS`, count how many `EQUNR` are associated with it.  
   - Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to `"KSA"` when a `HAUS` has less than 3 `EQUNR`.  
   - Else set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to `"EVU"`.

4. Join `J09B_temp` with table `EABLG` using the `INSTALLATION` field, extending `J09B_temp` with the needed columns from `EABLG`.

5. Join `J09B_temp` with tables `NET_MASTER` and `SALES_MASTER` using the `EQUNR` field (JOIN table with NET_MASTER and SALES_MASTER table using `EQUNR`), so that each row in `J09B_temp` has the corresponding customer data from both master tables.

6. Compare, for each row in `J09B_temp`, the customer data fields sourced from `SALES_MASTER` and from `NET_MASTER` to determine whether sales customer data is present (i.e., the sales customer data is NOT empty).

7. For each row in `J09B_temp`, decide which customer data to use based on the comparison (XOR gateway “sales customer data NOT empty” / “else”):
   - if sales customer data is NOT empty for this row:  
     - Use the `SALES_MASTER` customer data table entries for this row as the final customer data (overwrite / fill the customer-related fields for export from `SALES_MASTER`).  
   - else:  
     - Use the `NET_MASTER` customer data table entries for this row as the final customer data (fill the customer-related fields for export from `NET_MASTER`).

8. Export the final data in `J09B_temp` as a CSV file named `J09B`, using exactly these column names (and this order) for the export (Use this column names, when exporting the file):  
   `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`, `HAUS`, `ANLAGE`, `ME_MA_ID`, `EQUNR`, `HOUSE_NUM1`, `ENTSCHEIDUNG`, `TITLE`, `FORENAME`, `SURNAME`, `STREET`, `STREETNO`, `POST_CODE1`, `CITY1`, `CITY2`, `EMAIL`.

9. Send an email with the exported CSV file to the EVU:  
   - Email address: `meter.readings@evu.com`  
   - Attach the CSV file located at `\<path_to_exported_csv_file\>`.

10. After the email has been sent successfully, delete the temporary working table `J09B_temp`.

11. End the process.

process_rule (TEXT) NOT NULL - Process rule text/description"}

args.sqlite_command: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table';
CREATE TABLE J09B_temp AS SELECT * FROM decision_table WHERE TOUR LIKE 'J09B%';
ALTER TABLE J09B_temp ADD COLUMN ENTSCHEIDUNG TEXT;
UPDATE J09B_temp SET ENTSCHEIDUNG = CASE WHEN HAUS IN (SELECT HAUS FROM J09B_temp GROUP BY HAUS HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT EQUNR) < 3) THEN 'KSA' ELSE 'EVU' END;
ALTER TABLE J09B_temp ADD COLUMN "Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No." TEXT;
UPDATE J09B_temp SET "Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No." = (SELECT eablg."Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No." FROM eablg WHERE eablg.Installation = J09B_temp.ANLAGE LIMIT 1);
ALTER TABLE J09B_temp ADD COLUMN TITLE TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09B_temp ADD COLUMN FORENAME TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09B_temp ADD COLUMN SURNAME TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09B_temp ADD COLUMN STREET TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09B_temp ADD COLUMN STREETNO TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09B_temp ADD COLUMN CITY1_cust TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09B_temp ADD COLUMN CITY2_cust TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09B_temp ADD COLUMN POST_CODE1_cust INTEGER;
ALTER TABLE J09B_temp ADD COLUMN EMAIL TEXT;

UPDATE J09B_temp SET TITLE = (SELECT s.TITLE FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1), FORENAME = (SELECT s.FORENAME FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1), SURNAME = (SELECT s.SURNAME FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1), STREET = (SELECT s.STREET FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1), STREETNO = (SELECT s.STREETNO FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1), CITY1_cust = (SELECT s.CITY1 FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1), CITY2_cust = (SELECT s.CITY2 FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1), POST_CODE1_cust = (SELECT s.POST_CODE1 FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1), EMAIL = (SELECT s.EMAIL FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1);

UPDATE J09B_temp SET TITLE = COALESCE((SELECT s.TITLE FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1),(SELECT n.TITLE FROM net_master n WHERE n.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1)), FORENAME = COALESCE((SELECT s.FORENAME FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1),(SELECT n.FORENAME FROM net_master n WHERE n.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1)), SURNAME = COALESCE((SELECT s.SURNAME FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1),(SELECT n.SURNAME FROM net_master n WHERE n.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1)), STREET = COALESCE((SELECT s.STREET FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1),(SELECT n.STREET FROM net_master n WHERE n.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1)), STREETNO = COALESCE((SELECT s.STREETNO FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1),(SELECT n.STREETNO FROM net_master n WHERE n.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1)), CITY1_cust = COALESCE((SELECT s.CITY1 FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1),(SELECT n.CITY1 FROM net_master n WHERE n.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1)), CITY2_cust = COALESCE((SELECT s.CITY2 FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1),(SELECT n.CITY2 FROM net_master n WHERE n.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1)), POST_CODE1_cust = COALESCE((SELECT s.POST_CODE1 FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1),(SELECT n.POST_CODE1 FROM net_master n WHERE n.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1)), EMAIL = COALESCE((SELECT s.EMAIL FROM sales_master s WHERE s.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1),(SELECT n.EMAIL FROM net_master n WHERE n.EQUNR = J09B_temp.EQUNR LIMIT 1));

args.sql_query: SELECT "Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.", HAUS, ANLAGE, ME_MA_ID, EQUNR, HOUSE_NUM1, ENTSCHEIDUNG, TITLE, FORENAME, SURNAME, STREET, STREETNO, POST_CODE1_cust AS POST_CODE1, CITY1_cust AS CITY1, CITY2_cust AS CITY2, EMAIL FROM J09B_temp;

args.email:
Dear EVU team,

please find attached the meter reading data for tour J09B.

Best regards,
Your Automated Meter Reading System

kwargs.content:
The process for `process_rule_id 'J09B'` has been executed end-to-end:

- Created working table `J09B_temp` from all `decision_table` rows where `TOUR` starts with `J09B`.
- Added `ENTSCHEIDUNG` and classified per `HAUS`:
  - `KSA` if a house has fewer than 3 distinct `EQUNR`
  - `EVU` otherwise
- Joined in meter reading document data from `EABLG` via `ANLAGE` → `Installation` into `"Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No."`.
- Pulled customer data from `SALES_MASTER` and `NET_MASTER` via `EQUNR`, using:
  - `SALES_MASTER` data when present
  - otherwise `NET_MASTER` data
- Exported the final dataset to CSV `J09B.csv` with the required columns and order:
  - `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`, `HAUS`, `ANLAGE`, `ME_MA_ID`, `EQUNR`, `HOUSE_NUM1`, `ENTSCHEIDUNG`, `TITLE`, `FORENAME`, `SURNAME`, `STREET`, `STREETNO`, `POST_CODE1`, `CITY1`, `CITY2`, `EMAIL`
- Sent an email with this CSV attached to `meter.readings@evu.com`.
- Dropped the temporary table `J09B_temp`.

If you’d like, I can show you the exact SQL used or a sample of the exported rows.
